target.name: Beacon AI
target.founder: Matt Cox, Avinash Nair, Andrew Musto

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI

data.narrative:
Beacon AI is a Series A–stage AI & Software company founded by Matt Cox, Avinash Nair, and Andrew Musto, having raised $15M+ to date. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with primary stage-matched peers including Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $15M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to subsequent stage, implying $30M. Peer-Median triangulates across the three same-sector, same-stage comps, yielding a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous company maturity and investor sentiment within frontier AI.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $26M (conservative floor anchored to stage-progression) to $2.3B (peer maximum). The median implied valuation is $1.0B. Key uncertainties driving the wide band include: variance in peer company traction and defensibility, lack of Beacon AI–specific revenue or growth metrics, and stage-progression methodology's reliance on generic multipliers rather than company-specific fundamentals. Resolution of these uncertainties would require access to Beacon AI's financial performance and product development trajectory.
